q: A man starts from point A and walks 10 km north. He then turns right and walks 6 km. After that, he turns right again and walks 10 km. How far and in which direction is he from the starting point?
DESCRIBE: After moving north 10 km, east 6 km, and south 10 km, he ends up 6 km east of the starting point.

q: Ravi walks 8 km towards East, then 6 km towards South, and then 8 km towards West. How far is he from his starting point?
DESCRIBE: East and West movements cancel out. Final position is 6 km south of starting point.

q: A person walks 12 m north, then 5 m east, then 12 m south. In which direction is he from the starting point?
DESCRIBE: North and south movements cancel. He is 5 m east of the starting point.

q: A man walks 15 km towards North, then turns left and walks 9 km, then turns left again and walks 15 km. How far is he from the starting point?
DESCRIBE: After returning south 15 km, only 9 km west displacement remains.

q: Sita walks 7 km east, then 24 km north. What is the shortest distance from her starting point?
DESCRIBE: Using Pythagoras theorem: √(7² + 24²) = √625 = 25 km.

q: A person moves 20 m south, turns left and walks 15 m, then turns left and walks 20 m. In which direction is he from the starting point?
DESCRIBE: South → East → North. Final position is 15 m east of the starting point.

q: Rahul starts facing North. He turns 135° clockwise and then 90° anti-clockwise. Which direction is he facing now?
DESCRIBE: North +135° = South-East. Then -90° = North-East.

q: A person walks 10 km east, then 10 km south, then 10 km west, and finally 10 km north. Where is he now?
DESCRIBE: All movements cancel each other. Returns to starting point.

q: Mohan walks 13 km north and then 84 km east. What is the shortest distance between his initial and final positions?
A man is facing West. He turns 270° clockwise. Which direction is he facing now?
DESCRIBE: West → North → East → South after 270° clockwise turn.

q: A boy walks 6 km north, then 8 km east. How far is he from the starting point?
A person walks 5 m south, then 12 m east. What is the shortest distance from the starting point?
A man walks 30 m towards North, then 40 m towards East. What is the shortest distance from the starting point?
Ramesh starts facing South. He turns right, then left, then left. Which direction is he facing now?
DESCRIBE: South → West → South → East.

q: A person walks 9 km east, 12 km north and then 9 km west. How far is he from the starting point?
DESCRIBE: East and west cancel. Final displacement is 12 km north.

q: A man walks 4 km north, then 3 km east, then 4 km south. In which direction is he from the starting point?
DESCRIBE: North and south cancel. He remains 3 km east.

q: A person faces East. He turns 45° clockwise, then 180° clockwise. Which direction is he facing?
DESCRIBE: East → South-East → North-West.

q: A man walks 8 km west, then 15 km north. What is the shortest distance from the starting point?
A person moves 14 m east, then 48 m north. What is the shortest distance from the starting point?
Starting from a point, a person walks 25 m north, then 20 m east, then 25 m south. How far and in which direction is he from the starting point?
DESCRIBE: North and south cancel. Final position is 20 m east of the starting point.
